behavior_summary: This script governs an NPC guard (GUARD4) handling examine/U8P_GUARD4::look, area entry, scheduled patrol/reset, and player conversation.
U8P_GUARD4::look: when a player inspects the guard it prepares the object state, checks whether the NPC is dead, and emits either a 'dead guardsman' or 'guardsman' bark.

U8P_GUARD4::enterFastArea: triggered when an actor enters the guard's fast trigger. If the guard is dead it clears dead/in-combat state and teleports the guard to a fixed respawn position; otherwise it spawns short reaction behaviors (movement/animation).
U8P_GUARD4::schedule: periodic/scheduled routine that spawns a patrol/schedule method and, depending on the spawned process result, may clear the dead flag and spawn a reset/respawn method.

U8P_GUARD4::use: main talk/interaction handler. Plays greeting barks, updates local interaction counters influenced by global world-state flags (hydrosFree, hasHeart, pyrosFree), branches into panic/urgent dialog when appropriate, tracks first-meet/annoyed/thank states (guard4Met, guard4Pissed, guard4Thanked), builds a question menu (Who are you?, What is your duty?, Where am I?, Bye) using Item::ask, and responds to player choices with barks and spawned follow-up behaviors.
notable_control_flow: U8P_GUARD4::look: prepare/exclude object -> call Npc::isDead(self) -> if dead bark 'dead guardsman' else bark 'guardsman' -> suspend/return.

enterFastArea: prepare/exclude object -> call Npc::isDead(self) -> if dead: clear flags Npc::clrDead + Npc::clrInCombat and Npc::teleport to fixed coords; else spawn short reaction METHODS -> suspend.
schedule: spawn a patrol METHOD and inspect the spawned process result (dword->word). If the result is zero, call Npc::clrDead(self) and spawn a reset METHOD.

use: bark greeting and spawn helper/free routines -> build local interaction_count by reading hydrosFree/hasHeart/pyrosFree -> if count > 1 trigger an urgent bark and spawn METHOD::1090 -> otherwise possibly choose a random greeting (urandom) and check guard4Met/guard4Pissed to select tone -> assemble slist and call Item::ask -> branch on Item::ask return string to give specific answers, set/clear guard4Met/guard4Pissed/guard4Thanked, and spawn animation/cleanup behaviors.
open_questions: Exact semantics and internal behavior of spawned METHODS (e.g., METHOD::091C, METHOD::15E7, METHOD::143A, METHOD::133F, METHOD::0BCF, METHOD::1090) are not present — they likely control movement/animation/scheduling but specifics are unknown.
Precise scheduling cadence and conditions that trigger U8P_GUARD4::schedule and how schedule's spawned method returns the specific process result word used to decide a reset are not explicit.
The roles of FREE::1C19 and FREE::2A56 spawned from use() are inferred as delayed/cleanup helpers but their concrete effects and timing are unclear.

main_function.name: U8P_GUARD4::look
main_function.purpose: Handle player inspection of the guard: prepare info/exclusion, query whether the NPC is dead, and produce the appropriate short descriptive bark ('dead guardsman' vs 'guardsman').
entry_conditions: Event 0: invoked when the player 'looks at' or examines the guard process.

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD4::look
function_map.role: Examine/U8P_GUARD4::look handler for the guard; produces descriptive bark and checks dead state.

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD4::enterFastArea
function_map.role: Area-entry fast-trigger: respawn/relocate dead guard or spawn immediate reaction behaviors for living guard.

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD4::schedule
function_map.role: Periodic/scheduled maintenance: spawn patrol/schedule behavior and optionally perform reset/respawn based on spawned result.

function_map.name: U8P_GUARD4::use
function_map.role: Main interaction/talk handler: greeting/panic logic, dialog menu via Item::ask, and state updates for guard4Met/guard4Pissed/guard4Thanked.

external_calls.purpose: Query whether this NPC is dead to choose examine bark.
Emit the short descriptive string to the player ('dead guardsman' or 'guardsman').
Decide whether to respawn/relocate the guard or play reaction behaviors.
Clear dead flag to allow respawn/reset when area entry triggers recovery.
Clear in-combat state as part of reset/respawn.
Teleport the guard to fixed coordinates/orientation as part of respawn/relocation.
Spawn immediate short reaction behaviors (movement/animation) when an actor enters the guard's area (guard is alive).
Spawn patrol/schedule behavior to drive NPC movement or timed state changes.
Clear dead flag when the schedule's spawned result indicates a reset condition.
Spawn reset/respawn behavior as part of schedule recovery path.
Emit greeting, panic, and answer lines to the player during conversation.
Spawn animation/movement/reaction behaviors as dialog side-effects (METHOD::1090 used for panic/urgent response).
Spawned helper/cleanup/delayed routines invoked in use(); likely handle timing or resource cleanup after dialog.
Present dialog choices to player (Who are you?, What is your duty?, Where am I?, Bye) and return the selected option.
Choose between alternate greeting lines randomly.

reads.name: hydrosFree
reads.likely_effect: Influences the interaction_count in use(); contributes to panic/urgent dialog branching.

reads.name: hasHeart
reads.likely_effect: Influences interaction_count and dialog branching; used to pick alternate panic/greeting lines.

reads.name: pyrosFree
reads.likely_effect: Influences interaction_count in use(); contributes to panic/urgent dialog branching.

reads.name: devonInRule
reads.likely_effect: Used to choose between alternate duty/place reply lines in use()'s duty answer branch.

reads.name: guard4Met
reads.likely_effect: Read to determine first-meet vs repeated greeting behavior in use().

reads.name: guard4Pissed
reads.likely_effect: Read to choose shorter/angrier replies; affects greeting tone.

reads.name: guard4Thanked
reads.likely_effect: Read to determine whether the guard has already thanked the player (prevents repeated thanks).

writes.name: guard4Met
writes.likely_effect: Set to 1 when the player first uses/talks to the guard to mark 'met' status.

writes.name: guard4Pissed
writes.likely_effect: Cleared or set in different dialog branches to track the guard's annoyance state with the player.

writes.name: guard4Thanked
writes.likely_effect: Set to 1 when the player says 'Bye' and the guard thanks them to prevent repeated thanking.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script governs an NPC guard (GUARD4) handling examine/U8P_GUARD4::look, area entry, scheduled patrol/reset, and player conversation.
